leadText: If you were looking for the NPC living in Saffron City, see Copycat (character). For the TCG card featuring said character, see Copycat (Expedition 138). Copycat (Japanese: まねっこ Imitate) is a non-damaging Normal-type move introduced in Generation IV.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation IV 1.2 Generations V and VI 1.3 Generation VII onwards 1.4 Uncallable moves Effect [ edit | edit source ] This move effect may be in need of research. Reason: What effects do the Choice items have from Generation V on? You can discuss this on the talk page. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Copycat causes the user to use the last move that was used in the battle (even if the last move was by the user). If no move has been made, Copycat fails. If the copied move's target is not the user, an appropriate target is randomly chosen. The last-used move is updated whenever any Pokémon attempts to use a move. If a move successfully executes (generally, if the text " used !" is shown), that move becomes the last-used move. This is regardless of the move's result — whether it hit or missed or its effect was successful or failed (such as a Light Screen that fails because the previous Light Screen has not expired). Some moves that require multiple turns to execute do not count as valid moves in all of their turns. If Copycat executes after a turn when a move was not considered valid, Copycat will fail. Charging moves : the initial turn does not count as a valid move. Recharging moves : the recharge turn does not count as a valid move. Bide : the final turn does not count as a valid move. If any move calls another , the calling move is counted as the last move used instead of the called move. However, this only applies to the turn when the calling move was selected to be used. If a called move requires multiple turns to execute in full (such as Bide , Dig , or Thrash ), it may validly become the last-used move in subsequent turns. If a Pokémon tries to use a move but is prevented by a status condition or effect (such as sleep , paralysis , or flinching ), the last-used move becomes invalid and Copycat will fail if it executes next. Generations V and VI [ edit | edit source ] If any move successfully calls another, the called move now counts as the last move used instead of the calling move. The last move used is now unaffected if a Pokémon tries to use a move but is prevented by a status condition or effect. In other words, the last-used move remains what it was before the Pokémon's turn. Charging moves , recharging moves , and Bide no longer have invalid turns that will cause Copycat to fail. Instead, charging moves and Bide can be copied on any turn, while recharging moves are ignored on their recharge turn (the last move used remains whatever it was before the recharge message appeared). Generation VII onwards [ edit | edit source ] If powered up by a Normalium Z into Z-Copycat, the user's accuracy rises one stage. Additionally, if the move to be copied isn't a status move, then it will become its corresponding Z-Move . Copycat fails if the most recently used move is a Z-Move, regardless of whether Copycat was powered up into Z-Copycat or not. If the most recently used move is a Max Move , the user will copy the base move that was powered up. For example, if a Dynamax Pokémon used Max Guard by selecting a move slot that originally contained Trick Room , and the next Pokémon to move used Copycat, it would copy Trick Room, rather than copying Max Guard or failing entirely. Uncallable moves [ edit | edit source ] This move effect may be in need of research. Reason: Uncallable moves in Generation VIII; Sketch in BDSP You can discuss this on the talk page. Copycat will fail if any of the following moves is the last move used: Move Type Cat Generations IV V VI VII VIII Assist Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Baneful Bunker Poison Status ✔ ✔ Beak Blast Flying Physical ✔ ✔ Behemoth Bash Steel Physical ✔ Behemoth Blade Steel Physical ✔ Bestow Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Celebrate Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ Chatter Flying Special ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Circle Throw Fighting Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Copycat Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Counter Fighting Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Covet Normal Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Destiny Bond Ghost Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Detect Fighting Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Dragon Tail Dragon Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Dynamax Cannon Dragon Special ✔ Endure Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Feint Normal Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Focus Punch Fighting Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Follow Me Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Helping Hand Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Hold Hands Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ King's Shield Steel Status ✔ ✔ ✔ Mat Block Fighting Status ✔ ✔ ✔ Me First Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Metronome Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Mimic Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Mirror Coat Psychic Special ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Mirror Move Flying Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Protect Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Rage Powder Bug Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Roar Normal Status ✘ ✘ ✔ ✔ ✔ Shell Trap Fire Special ✔ ✔ Sketch Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Sleep Talk Normal Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Snatch Dark Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Struggle Normal Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Spiky Shield Grass Status ✔ ✔ ✔ Spotlight Normal Status ✔ Switcheroo Dark Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Thief Dark Physical ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Transform Normal Status ✘ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Trick Psychic Status ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ Whirlwind Normal Status ✘ ✘ ✔ ✔ ✔ Additionally, Copycat fails if the last move used was a Z-Move.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS PBR B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The user mimics the move used immediately before it. The move fails if no other move has been used yet.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Causes the user to unleash the same move as the last one used by the facing enemy. Some moves can't be copied. B S L しょうめんの てきポケモンが さいごに くりだしたわざと おなじ わざをくりだす いくつか まねっこできないわざも ある MD GtI UNUSED S MD You counter the enemy by mimicking the enemy's last move. MD RT DX You counter the enemy by mimicking the enemy's last move. There are some moves that can't be used in this way.
